title: Pork Dumpling Sando
summary: Juicy and flavoursome pork dumpling filling, sandwiched between two slices of crunchy white bread, coated in sesame seeds. Like a prawn toast and pork dumpling hybrid.

method.description: Trim and thinly slice the spring onions, then transfer to a bowl with the rest of the filling ingredients. Stir together until the mixture changes consistency and becomes sticky.
Divide the mixture between four slices of bread, then evenly smooth out. Top each one with another slice of plain bread to make sandwiches, then cut the crusts off.

Spread some mayo over the top of each sandwich. Tip the sesame seeds onto a plate or baking tray. Place the sandwiches into the seeds, mayo-side down, and gently pat so the sesame seeds stick. Spread mayo onto the other side of the sandwiches, then flip and coat in sesame seeds so all sides of each sandwich are coated in seeds.
Add a generous drizzle of oil to a frying pan and place over a medium-high heat. Once hot, add the sandwiches and cook on both sides until golden brown (4-5 mins in total).
Transfer the sandwiches to a baking sheet and bake at 200C for 10-15 mins until cooked through. Slice into quarters or triangles before serving.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
